question.text: Lakshmi bought units of an equity mutual fund at NAV of INR 100. After one year, the NAV is INR 108 and she received a dividend of INR 4 per unit during the year. What is Lakshmi's Holding Period Return (HPR)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** HPR = (Income + End Value - Begin Value) / Begin Value = (4 + 108 - 100) / 100 = 12 / 100 = 12.00%. HPR captures both capital appreciation (8%) and income (4%) as a percentage of the initial investment. **The Trap:** 8% considers only capital appreciation (108 - 100)/100 and ignores the dividend income. 4% considers only dividend yield (4/100). 10% is a common miscalculation. Many candidates forget that HPR must include ALL sources of return — both income and capital gains. **Cross-Exam Context:** HPR is the most basic return measure tested across all NISM exams — Series V-A, X-A, X-B, XII, and XV. It forms the building block for TWRR and MWRR calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always include dividends, bonuses, and other distributions when calculating returns for clients. Reporting only NAV appreciation understates the true return and misrepresents fund performance.
